name: MicroPython (Python for Microcontrollers)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Damien George / micropython contributors

auth.notes: Firmware for microcontrollers; no cloud auth

performance.notes: Bare metal execution; much faster than CPython for embedded

agent_readiness.notes: Python subset for microcontrollers (RP2040, ESP32, STM32, etc.). from machine import Pin, PWM, ADC, I2C, SPI, UART. led = Pin(25, Pin.OUT); led.on(); led.off(); led.toggle(). PWM: pwm = PWM(Pin(15)); pwm.freq(1000); pwm.duty_u16(32768). ADC: adc = ADC(Pin(26)); adc.read_u16(). I2C: i2c = I2C(0, sda=Pin(20), scl=Pin(21)); i2c.scan(). UART: uart = UART(0, 9600); uart.write('hello'). asyncio: import asyncio; async def main(): await asyncio.sleep(1). WiFi (ESP32): import network; wlan = network.WLAN(); wlan.connect('ssid', 'password'). Bootloader via USB or UART. mpremote for deployment. Boards: Raspberry Pi Pico, ESP32, ESP8266, Pyboard, BBC micro:bit.

gotchas: Not all Python stdlib available — check micropython-lib for extras
float is 32-bit by default on some boards (not 64-bit like CPython)
Memory constrained — typical boards have 256KB-512KB RAM
No pip — use mpremote or Thonny for library management
Some Python patterns (generators, closures) have limitations
best_when: Embedded hardware projects on ESP32, RP2040, ESP8266
IoT sensors, actuators, displays with Python-like scripting
When Arduino C++ is too low-level but full Python is too heavy
avoid_when: When CircuitPython's beginner-friendliness or library ecosystem is preferred
Complex ML inference (use dedicated edge ML instead)
summary: MicroPython runs Python on microcontrollers (ESP32, RP2040, STM32). machine.Pin, PWM, ADC, I2C, SPI, UART. asyncio for cooperative multitasking. WiFi on ESP32. mpremote for deployment. MIT. Memory constrained — 256KB-512KB RAM typical. Not all stdlib available. Best for ESP32/RP2040 IoT projects. CircuitPython for beginners or Adafruit hardware. Arduino for real-time control.
